page: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 16 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical AMD EPYC cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 960 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector extensions, AVX_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 10%. Multi-core stress-ng performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory performance is strong, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25%, while uncached large-block read and write bandwidths remain average at 89.11 GB/sec and 45.67 GB/sec, respectively. For lightweight machine learning workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 443.84 tokens/sec on a small 135M parameter model, though performance scales down to average levels for larger 7B parameter models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model offers a resource-dense configuration that balances high memory capacity and fast NVMe storage without the premium associated with dedicated CPU cores. This makes the server well-suited for workloads that require substantial memory and fast local storage but do not demand continuous, dedicated CPU execution. Suitable workloads include database operations, static web serving, redis caching layers, and lightweight text processing or small-scale language model inference.
description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is a general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 16 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC processor, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU and compression performance, alongside strong memory scores and average multi-core capabilities. The shared CPU model provides a cost-effective balance of high memory density and fast local storage. This server is suited for database hosting, static web serving, caching, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is a general-purpose server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced virtual server combining shared AMD EPYC processors, high memory density, and fast NVMe storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC processors with AVX-512 support
64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
960 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Suitable for database hosting, caching, and web serving workloads
